event: Gerard of Avesnes Exposed on the Walls of Arsuf
symbols: man tied to post on walls — the cruelty of using prisoners as human shields
fortress walls — the contested stronghold of Arsuf during the Crusades
assembled crowd below — the besieging army forced to witness their comrade's peril
description: A dramatic black and white engraving showing a man bound to a tall wooden post atop a stone fortress wall. Several figures sit or stand on the ramparts around him. Below the walls, a large crowd of armed men and observers gathers, looking up at the exposed prisoner. The fortress has massive stone blocks and towers. The sky is rendered with dramatic cloud formations and rays of light. The scene conveys a medieval siege setting.

_meta.title: Gerard of Avesnes exposed on the walls of Arsuf
